title: Vendor Metric
description: Pointer to a vendor-defined metric available on a product, beyond the closed `available-metric.json` enum. The seller declares 'I support this vendor's metric'; everything else (category, standard alignment, methodology, description, documentation) lives at the vendor — `brand.json` `agents[type='measurement']` is the canonical anchor, queried once per vendor (cacheable) when the buyer needs metadata. Used in `reporting_capabilities.vendor_metrics` for proprietary metrics not yet in the standard enum. The metric is identified by the tuple `(vendor, metric_id)`; identifiers are namespaced by the vendor, so the same `metric_id` may mean different things in different vendors' vocabularies. Promotion path: when the industry converges on a metric via a published standard, the spec adds it to the closed `available-metric.json` enum and the vendor extensions become historical aliases.

vendor.description: Vendor that defines and computes this metric. The vendor's `brand.json` is the discovery anchor for the measurement agent (entry with `type: 'measurement'` in the `agents[]` array), the metric's category and standard alignment, and human-readable methodology — none of those are duplicated inline here.

metric_id.description: Identifier for the metric within the vendor's vocabulary (e.g., `attention_units`, `gco2e_per_impression`, `demographic_reach`). Cross-vendor querying typically goes through `vendor` enumeration in the buyer agent (which knows or discovers which vendors offer e.g. attention measurement) rather than ID matching, since vendors rarely converge on identifiers before standardization.
